question: Will Rajasthan's State Election Commission announce the panchayat (PRI) general election schedule before July 25, 2026?

factors.description: OBC survey runs July 10-23; report compiled/submitted only after July 23; SEC schedule awaits reservation finalization
SEC has stated it needs at least 90 days after reservation process complete to conduct elections
Status quo: no schedule announced, repeated delays, reports of slip to Sept-Oct
HC pressure (July 31 deadline) plus fallback option creates small chance of a sudden announcement

summary: Rajasthan's OBC door-to-door survey runs July 10-23, 2026, with the reservation report compiled and submitted only AFTER July 23. The SEC has stated it needs ~90 days after reservation is finalized before conducting polls, and it explicitly awaits reservation notifications before announcing any schedule. With the prerequisite process not concluding until the very end of the resolution window, announcing an actual PRI polling date before 23:59 IST July 24 is highly improbable. Reports indicate polls may slip to September-October. Small residual probability remains for a surprise announcement under High Court pressure (July 31 deadline) using the fallback of treating OBC seats as general.
reasoning:
**Forecast: 5% (NO)**

The decisive fact is timing: Rajasthan's OBC reservation survey runs July 10–23, 2026, with the report compiled and submitted only *after* July 23. Since the SEC explicitly awaits reservation notifications before announcing a schedule—and has said it needs ~90 days lead time—there is virtually no path to announcing an actual PRI polling date within the July 11–24 window. The residual 5% accounts for a surprise announcement under High Court pressure using the fallback of treating OBC-reserved seats as general.
